questions.q: What is the primary purpose of using a Computerised Accounting System (CAS)?
questions.answer: The primary purpose of using a CAS is to improve the efficiency, accuracy, and reliability of accounting data.

questions.q: Identify the advantages of using a Cloud-based Accounting Software.
questions.answer: The advantages of using a Cloud-based Accounting Software are _______________________

questions.q: What is the difference between a Spreadsheet and a Database in an Accounting system?
questions.answer: A Spreadsheet is a two-dimensional table of data, whereas a Database is a collection of related data stored in a structured format.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of an Accounting Software that uses the Client-Server Architecture?
Explain the concept of Data Backup and Recovery in a Computerised Accounting System.
questions.answer: Data Backup and Recovery refer to the process of creating a copy of data and restoring it in case of a system failure or data loss.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The process of converting data from one format to another format is known as _______________________.
What is the purpose of using a Firewall in a Computerised Accounting System?
questions.answer: The purpose of using a Firewall is to prevent unauthorised access to the system and protect it from external threats.

questions.q: Identify the types of Computerised Accounting Systems.
questions.answer: The types of Computerised Accounting Systems are _______________________

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a Database Management System used in Accounting?
Explain the concept of Data Encryption in a Computerised Accounting System.
questions.answer: Data Encryption is the process of converting data into a secure format to protect it from unauthorised access.
